response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack web developer with strong software craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD) and some systems-level exposure (low-latency DLang services, C/C++ at National Instruments), but the resume shows no evidence of SoC/IP modeling, GPU or accelerator architecture, deep-learning workload analysis, or hardware-software co-design — the core requirements of a Principal Performance Architect role. While the years-of-experience bar is met with a BS in Computer Science, the domain gap between web/product engineering and silicon-level performance architecture is too large for this position.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching in DLang handling millions of requests/sec)
Broad, long-tenured engineering background spanning web, embedded, and desktop with strong testing discipline
Some C/C++ systems programming experience (NI LabWindows/CVI components, audio synthesis thesis work)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, chip architecture, or hardware performance-modeling experience
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or GPU/accelerator workload analysis background
Career trajectory centers on full-stack web/product development, not principal-level hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Low-latency systems and performance optimization exposure
